text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on. Cybersecurity names are leading tech higher, Alphabet popping on Dow debut adds broad tech tailwind. But a potentially weak jobs report this week is the real wildcard — bad macro data hits growth/tech names hard, and you're on margin.

Your holdings: AVGO (-14.94%) is your biggest concern — largest position, deep in the red, stop at 336, needs active watching on any tech pullback. PLTR (-11.41%) is flagged SELL — you're losing money and the system says act, don't wait for a bounce. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec bleeding the most percentage-wise — small size hopefully, but cut it if conviction is gone. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no track record — keep size tiny. Margin means these losses compound fast if the market turns.

Watch: Friday jobs report — a miss could slam tech/chips across the board. Watch AVGO price action near its stop (336) closely.

Bottom line: Your two biggest fires are PLTR (sell signal you haven't acted on) and AVGO (largest position underwater) — address those before the jobs report lands.
